title: NOC 21211 – Data Scientists | GTR Immigration
description: Data Scientists are among the most sought-after technology professionals across Canada, driving innovation in artificial intelligence, financial analytics, automation, healthcare research, and public-sector policy modelling. Classified under NOC 21211 (TEER 1), this occupation qualifies for skilled-worker immigration streams such as Express-Entry-aligned programs, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), LMIA-supported work permits, and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.major_group: 212 – Professional occupations in applied sciences (except engineering)
classification.minor_group: 2121 – Mathematicians, statisticians, actuaries, and data scientists

duties: Apply machine-learning, deep-learning, and AI techniques
Run large-scale experiments on complex datasets
Build regression, clustering, simulation, and neural-network models
Extract and prepare data using programming languages
Implement new statistical or mathematical methodologies
Visualize data for rapid business decision-making
Clean and organize structured and unstructured data
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in statistics, mathematics, computer science, engineering, or related field
Master’s or PhD in data science, machine learning, or quantitative disciplines (often preferred)
Strong programming skills (Python, R, SQL, etc.)
Experience in statistical modelling and machine learning
job_outlook: Between 2022–2031, Canada is projected to create 29,300 new positions, with approximately 32,700 job seekers—showing a competitive but immigration-friendly market, particularly for candidates with:
additional_information: Demand continues to surge nationwide due to:
AI adoption across government servicesDigital-banking expansionHealth-data researchAutomation and predictive modellingSmart-city and infrastructure analytics
Consulting firmsUniversities and research institutesBanks and financial institutionsGovernment departmentsTechnology companiesEnterprise analytics teams
Annual salaries generally range between $44,300–$108,600 CAD, depending on experience, province, and technical specialization.
